name: Segundo (SS-398)
events: 26 Jun 1944 With her trials and initial training completed USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. James D. Fulp, Jr.) departed from New London bound for Pearl Harbor.
25 Jul 1944 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) arrived at Pearl Harbor.

15 Aug 1944 From 15 to 17 August 1944, USS Seahorse (T/Capt. C.W. Wilkins, USN), USS Whale (T/Cdr. J.B. Grady, USN) and USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with an incoming convoy. During these exercises they were escorted by USS Lovering (Lt.Cdr. E.P. Bayley, USNR, with COMCORTDIV 10, Cdr. G.B. Coale, USNR on board) and USS Griswold (Lt.Cdr. M.C. Walley, USNR).
21 Aug 1944 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Philippines.

21 Aug 1944 Around 1330 VW/21, USS Seahorse (T/Capt. C.W. Wilkins, USN), USS Whale (T/Cdr. J.B. Grady, USN) and USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) departed Pearl Harbor for their sixth, ninth and first war patrols respectively. They were first to proceed to Saipan before continuing on to their patrol area where they were to operate as a coordinated attack group.
They were escorted out until 2000 VW by USS PC-1077 (Lt. G.B. Glave, USNR).
En-route to Saipan the submarine carried out warious exercises. (1)
3 Sep 1944 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. James D. Fulp, Jr.) arrived at Saipan to refuel, and then resumes her patrol later that day.

3 Sep 1944 Around 0530 K/3, USS Seahorse (T/Capt. C.W. Wilkins, USN), USS Whale (T/Cdr. J.B. Grady, USN) and USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) made rendezvous with USS Downes (T/Cdr. R.S. Fahle, USN).
They then proceeded to Tenapag harbour where they arrived around 1130 K/3.
The submarines were then taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Holland (Cdr. C.Q. Wright, Jr., USNR). (1)

4 Sep 1944 Around 0800 I/4, USS Seahorse (T/Capt. C.W. Wilkins, USN), USS Whale (T/Cdr. J.B. Grady, USN) and USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) departed Saipan to proceed to their patrol area to the east of the Philippines.
They were escorted out until around 1400 I/4, by USS Heed (Lt. M. Dent. Jr., USNR).
Around 1030 I/5, the submarines parted company to proceed independently to their patrol area. (1)

14 Sep 1944 Around 0715 I/14, the 'Bear' wolfpack, made up of USS Seahorse (T/Capt. C.W. Wilkins, USN), USS Whale (T/Cdr. J.B. Grady, USN) and USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) was joined by USS Grouper (T/Cdr. F.H. Wahlig, USN). (1)

25 Sep 1944 Around 0900 I/25, the 'Bear' wolfpack, made up of USS Seahorse (T/Capt. C.W. Wilkins, USN), USS Grouper (T/Cdr. F.H. Wahlig, USN), USS Whale (T/Cdr. J.B. Grady, USN) and USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea by ComSubPac.
USS Grouper left the 'Bear' Wolfpack on 28 September also by order of ComSubPac. (1)

30 Sep 1944 At 0230 H/30, USS Seahorse (T/Capt. C.W. Wilkins, USN) and USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) made rendezvous and additional patrol instructions were passed from HMS Seahorse to USS Segundo. (2)

1 Oct 1944 Around 1600 H/1, USS Seahorse (T/Capt. C.W. Wilkins, USN), USS Whale (T/Cdr. J.B. Grady, USN) and USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) were to form a patrol line off the north-west point of Luzon with USS Seahorse in position 18°50'N, 119°40'E and the other two submarines 15 miles to the west and east of her. (1)
21 Oct 1944 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) ended her 1st war patrol at Majuro.
16 Nov 1944 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) departed from Majuro for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in Luzon Strait.

6 Dec 1944 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese merchant cargo ships Kenjo Maru (6933 GRT), Yasukuni Maru (5794 GRT) and Shinto Maru (1215 GRT) west of Dalupiri Island, Philippines in position 18°57'N, 120°58'E.
Shinto Maru is sunk later that day by US aircraft, the Yasukuni Maru is beached and declared to total loss and the Kenjo Maru is sunk the next day by USS Trepang.
5 Jan 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) ended her 2nd war patrol at Guam.
1 Feb 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) departed from Guam for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the East China Sea.
9 Mar 1945 USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) attacked a Japanese convoy to the north-west of Amami-o-Shima in position 29°04'N, 128°28'E. Six torpedoes were fired but no hits were obtained.

This was convoy Kana 803 which was made up of the transports Dokan Maru (2270 GRT, built 1944), Keizan Maru (2308 GRT, built 1918) and Sanka Maru (2488 GRT, built 1918). It was on passage from Kagoshima, Kyushu, Japan to Naha, Okinawa and was escorted by the frigates Kaibokan 44, Kaibokan 118, auxiliary minesweeper Taihei Maru No.3 (197 GRT, built 1930) and auxiliary netlayer Shinto Maru No.2 (540 GRT, built 1944) (all links are offsite links).
It was Kaibokan 44 which depth charged USS Jallao at 2345 I/9.
All three transports were sunk the following day by USS Kete (T/Lt.Cdr. E. Ackerman, USN).

1752 I/9 - In the East China Sea, east of Yakushima, in position 30°29'N, 128°52'E, while dived conducting periscope patrol, the tops of three ships were sighted on bearing 153°. Headed towards and picked up one frigate and one submarine chaser with the targets which looked like three medium sized transports.

1920 I/9 - Surfaced. A contact report was sent to all boats in the area but only USS Segundo (T/Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr., USN) replied. Tracking had showed the course of the enemy as being 215° at 7.5 knots. A third escort was also seen on the radar screen.
2000 I/9 - Recognition signals and info were exchanged with the SJ radar with USS Kete who told USS Jallao that they would attack after her.
2313 I/9 - Dived to radar depth and commenced approach.
2338 I/9 - In position 29°04'N, 128°28'E (north-west of Amami-o-Shima), fired six bow torpedoes at the overlapping transports from 2490 yards.
2343 I/9 - Heard an explosion which was hoped to be a hit on a target.

2345 I/9 - Heard the first of 17 depth charges in three groups. One escort passed down the starboard side and dropped seven depth charges about one second apart. All three escorts came back and hunted the attacker until 0030 I/9 when only one escort was hunting.

0045 I/10 - HE was no longer heard. Maybee the escort had stopped as his screws faded out very quickly. Radar interference was picked up on the convoy's port bow and it was assumed USS Kete was making her attack run but no sound of her making an attack was picked up.

0235 I/10 - Surfaced after having reloaded the torpedo tubes. The seas were very rough. Headed down the base course of the convoy. A large quantity of water entered the main induction. As the convoy was now about 25 miles away and the likelihood of completing an end around before dawn the case was quickly given up. Informed USS Balao (T/Lt.Cdr. R.K.R. Worthington, USN), which was to the south-west of USS Jallao of the bearing and distance of the convoy. Also informed her that USS Kete was also in contact with the convoy.

0403 I/10 - Sighted the red glow of a probable explosion on bearing 220° which was the general bearing of the convoy. USS Kete was asked USS Jallao did any damage. She replied that no damage had been done by USS Jallao.
0610 I/10 - Submerged and ran towards Gajashima which was sighted at 1829 I/10. USS Jallao then left patrol by transiting the Nansei Shoto to the south of Suwanosejima. (3)
11 Mar 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Shori Maru (3087 GRT) off southern Korea in position 34°25'N, 127°54'E.
26 Mar 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) ended her 3rd war patrol at Pearl Harbor. In late April she departed for her 4th war patrol in the East China and Yellow Seas.

22 Apr 1945 USS Capitaine (Lt.Cdr. E.S. Friedrick, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Pruitt (Lt. D.B. Harby, USNR), USS PGM-21 (Lt. L. Gardiner, USNR), USS Muskalunge (Lt.Cdr. L.A. Lajaunie, Jr., USN) and USS Segundo (Cdr. James D. Fulp, Jr., USN).

23 Apr 1945 USS Capitaine (Lt.Cdr. E.S. Friedrick, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Pruitt (Lt. D.B. Harby, USNR), USS PGM-21 (Lt. L. Gardiner, USNR), USS Muskalunge (Lt.Cdr. L.A. Lajaunie, Jr., USN) and USS Segundo (Cdr. James D. Fulp, Jr., USN).
29 May 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) sank 7 Japanese sailing vessels with gunfire in the Yellow Sea in position 37°08'N, 125°24'E.
3 Jun 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) sank the Japanese merchant sailing vessel Anto Maru No.94 off Jinsen, Korea in position 36°41'N, 125°23'E.
7 Jun 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) sank a small Japanese ship with gunfire in the Yellow Sea in position 37°42'N, 124°02'E.
9 Jun 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. J.D. Fulp, Jr.) sank two small Japanese ships with gunfire in the Yellow Sea in position 39°08'N, 123°10'E.

11 Jun 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. James D. Fulp, Jr.) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Fukui Maru No.2 (1591 GRT) in the Yellow Sea off the Shantung peninsula in position 37°11'N, 123°23'E.
10 Aug 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. Stephen L. Johnson) departed from Midway for her 5th war patrol, and was ordered to patrol in the Sea of Okhotsk.

29 Aug 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. S.L. Johnson) encounters the Japanese submarine I-401 off the northeast coast of Honshu, and after negotiations placed a prize crew on board the Japanese submarine. Both submarines then proceeded to Tokyo Bay.
31 Aug 1945 USS Segundo (Lt.Cdr. S.L. Johnson) enters Tokyo Bay.
